channel.title: Mathematics Research Blog
channel.description: Elias Judin is a research mathematician working in category theory, algebra, and formalized mathematics.

item.title: Topological, Lax-Topological (Semi-Topological/Solid), and Topologically Algebraic Functors
item.description: Notes on topological, lax-topological (semi-topological/solid), and topologically algebraic functors via structured sources/sinks, classical characterizations, and the quantaloid-enriched “topological = total” theorem.

item.title: Reflections on the Right Use of Artificial Intelligence With a View to the Love of Mathematics
item.description: An essay on AI-generated mathematics through Meillassoux’s arche-fossil and Simone Weil’s account of attention.

item.title: Tarski's automorphisms
item.description: Notes on Tarski’s proposal that logical notions are those invariant under every automorphism of the universe of discourse.

item.title: Sheaves as Presheaves with an Equalizer Diagram
item.description: The sheaf axioms and their formulation as an equalizer diagram.

item.title: Monomorphisms and epimorphisms
item.description: Some basic properties and examples of monomorphisms, epimorphisms, and split morphisms.

item.title: hom-Sets
item.description: Some calculations with hom-sets and the covariant and contravariant hom-functors.

item.title: Describing difficult spaces
item.description: Topological spaces described through gluing diagrams, identification spaces, and geometric models.

item.title: Free semimodules and their examples
item.description: My honours project on free semimodules and their examples.

item.title: M-sets and Yoneda for monoids
item.description: M-sets, the Yoneda lemma for monoids, and the Yoneda embedding.

item.title: Free algebras by means of a universal property
item.description: Free algebras and their universal property, beginning with free vector spaces.

item.title: Modules and Vector Spaces as Algebraic Categories
item.description: How modules and vector spaces can be described as algebraic categories.

item.title: Groupoids and skeletons of monoids and preorders
item.description: Monoids and preorders as categories, and the corresponding groupoids and skeletons.

item.title: Exercises in category theory
item.description: Series of posts containing short answers to exercises in category theory from my honours year at the University of Cape Town

item.title: Is the Double Twisted Möbius Strip Isotopic to the Strip?
item.description: A Hopf-link obstruction to an isotopy between the double-twisted Möbius strip and the ordinary strip.
